title: Schismatoglottis calyptrata
taxonomy: Schismatoglottis calyptrata is classified within the family Arumaceae and the order Alismatales. This classification places it among the flowering plants, more specifically within the monocots. As a member of the Arumaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants such as the Philodendron and the Dieffenbachia. The family Arumaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Schismatoglottis calyptrata being part of the Lasioideae subfamily. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Schismatoglottis calyptrata with other related plants.

intro: Schismatoglottis calyptrata, also known as the umbrella-leaf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Schismatoglottis calyptrata include various alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being made into ointments and creams, to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
